mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an economic contraction that triggers corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional rank correlation between unemployment changes and SPX returns exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth outpacing consumer price inflation indicates healthy demand expansion without price pressures, which improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (GDPC1 growth minus CPI growth) shows a negative rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are flat when real growth exceeds inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production accelerates equity returns during late-cycle expansions as capacity utilization peaks before demand contraction, providing a short-term momentum boost to the S&P 500 over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns decline when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in expansionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 700 thousand units reflects a severe credit crunch and wealth effect contraction, which suppresses consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of instances where housing starts drop below 700, or if the cross-sectional correlation between housing declines and equity gains is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm labor market healing, which reduces recession probabilities and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when payrolls rise and unemployment falls.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes indicate expanding consumer demand that directly supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward returns remain negative when retail sales rise month-over-month, or the signal's predictive rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional rank correlation between inflation spikes and SPX returns exceeds 0.05.
